content.thinking: Let me start by inspecting the current configuration of the spark-pi namespace to understand what's there before creating the migration plan.

content.text: I'll inspect the current configuration of the spark-pi namespace first, then write a migration plan.

input.command: kubectl get deploy spark-master spark-worker -n spark-pi -o yaml 2>&1
input.description: Get deployment configs for spark-master and spark-worker
Get all jobs in spark-pi namespace

content.content:
apiVersion: v1
items:
- apiVersion: batch/v1
  kind: Job
  metadata:
    annotations:
      kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
        {"apiVersion":"batch/v1","kind":"Job","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"spark-pi","namespace":"spark-pi"},"spec":{"backoffLimit":0,"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"spark-pi","component":"driver"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["/bin/bash","-c","echo \"==============================================\"\necho \"  SparkPi Job\"\necho \"==============================================\"\nexport SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)\n/opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \\\n  --master local[2] \\\n  --driver-memory 512m \\\n  --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \\\n  /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \\\n  1000\nEXIT_CODE=$?\necho \"SparkPi job finished with exit code: $EXIT_CODE\"\nexit $EXIT_CODE\n"],"image":"apache/spark:3.5.3","name":"spark-submit","resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"1Gi"},"requests":{"cpu":"200m","memory":"512Mi"}}}],"restartPolicy":"Never","serviceAccountName":"spark-pi"}}}}
    creationTimestamp: "2026-06-24T10:07:37Z"
    generation: 1
    labels:
      app: spark-pi
      batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: f7ce62cb-0526-409b-8ece-e99a1956e36d
      batch.kubernetes.io/job-name: spark-pi
      component: driver
      controller-uid: f7ce62cb-0526-409b-8ece-e99a1956e36d
      job-name: spark-pi
    name: spark-pi
    namespace: spark-pi
    resourceVersion: "975526"
    uid: f7ce62cb-0526-409b-8ece-e99a1956e36d
  spec:
    backoffLimit: 0
    completionMode: NonIndexed
    completions: 1
    manualSelector: false
    parallelism: 1
    podReplacementPolicy: TerminatingOrFailed
    selector:
      matchLabels:
        batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: f7ce62cb-0526-409b-8ece-e99a1956e36d
    suspend: false
    template:
      metadata:
        creationTimestamp: null
        labels:
          app: spark-pi
          batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: f7ce62cb-0526-409b-8ece-e99a1956e36d
          batch.kubernetes.io/job-name: spark-pi
          component: driver
          controller-uid: f7ce62cb-0526-409b-8ece-e99a1956e36d
          job-name: spark-pi
      spec:
        containers:
        - command:
          - /bin/bash
          - -c
          - |
            echo "=============================================="
            echo "  SparkPi Job"
            echo "=============================================="
            export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
            /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
              --master local[2] \
              --driver-memory 512m \
              --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
              /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
              1000
            EXIT_CODE=$?
            echo "SparkPi job finished with exit code: $EXIT_CODE"
            exit $EXIT_CODE
          image: apache/spark:3.5.3
          imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
          name: spark-submit
          resources:
            limits:
              cpu: 500m
              memory: 1Gi
            requests:
              cpu: 200m
              memory: 512Mi
          terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
          terminationMessagePolicy: File
        dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
        restartPolicy: Never
        schedulerName: default-scheduler
        securityContext: {}
        serviceAccount: spark-pi
        serviceAccountName: spark-pi
        terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
  status:
    completionTime: "2026-06-24T10:10:14Z"
    conditions:
    - lastProbeTime: "2026-06-24T10:10:14Z"
      lastTransitionTime: "2026-06-24T10:10:14Z"
      message: Reached expected number of succeeded pods
      reason: CompletionsReached
      status: "True"
      type: SuccessCriteriaMet
    - lastProbeTime: "2026-06-24T10:10:14Z"
      lastTransitionTime: "2026-06-24T10:10:14Z"
      message: Reached expected number of succeeded pods
      reason: CompletionsReached
      status: "True"
      type: Complete
    ready: 0
    startTime: "2026-06-24T10:07:38Z"
    succeeded: 1
    terminating: 0
    uncountedTerminatedPods: {}
kind: List
metadata:
  resourceVersion: ""

content.content:
apiVersion: v1
data:
  findings.txt: |-
    SPARK-PI NAMESPACE COMPLIANCE AUDIT
    Date: 2026-06-24
    Auditor: automated read-only inspection

    ================================================================
    SUMMARY
    ================================================================
    Compliance area              | Status
    -----------------------------|--------
    RBAC / least-privilege       | PARTIAL PASS (1 gap)
    Resource limits              | PASS (namespace quota gap)
    Image provenance             | PARTIAL PASS (2 gaps)
    Security contexts (pod/ctr)  | FAIL (missing across all workloads)
    Network policies             | FAIL (none defined)
    Job configuration            | PASS

    ================================================================
    SECTION 1 — RBAC
    ================================================================
    PASS: Dedicated service account 'spark-pi' created; workloads do
          not use the 'default' service account.

    PASS: Namespace-scoped Role 'spark-pi-role' used (not a
          ClusterRole), binding enforced via RoleBinding
          'spark-pi-role-binding'.

    PASS: Only the 'spark-pi' service account is bound to the role.

    GAP-1 (RBAC): The live Role 'spark-pi-role' grants full
          create/get/list/watch/delete/update/patch on 'pods', which
          was NOT present in the originally applied manifest recorded
          in the last-applied-configuration annotation. The annotation
          shows the original intent was only services, configmaps, and
          persistentvolumeclaims. Post-apply drift has expanded pod
          permissions beyond what the original spec intended.
          Current live resources in role:
            - services
            - configmaps
            - persistentvolumeclaims
            - pods   <-- ADDED AFTER INITIAL APPLY (drift)
          Risk: the spark-pi service account can now create/delete
          arbitrary pods in the namespace, significantly expanding its
          blast radius.

    ================================================================
    SECTION 2 — RESOURCE LIMITS AND QUOTAS
    ================================================================
    PASS: All three workloads (spark-master Deployment, spark-worker
          Deployment, spark-pi Job) define both resource requests and
          limits on every container:
            - CPU:    requests=200m, limits=500m
            - Memory: requests=512Mi, limits=1Gi

    GAP-2 (RESOURCE QUOTAS): No LimitRange or ResourceQuota objects
          exist in the namespace. Without them, any future workload
          deployed into spark-pi could consume unbounded cluster
          resources. Namespace-level enforcement is absent.

    ================================================================
    SECTION 3 — IMAGE PROVENANCE
    ================================================================
    PASS: All containers use a pinned version tag (apache/spark:3.5.3)
          rather than ':latest'. The tag is consistent across all
          three workloads.

    GAP-3 (IMAGE DIGEST): Images are referenced by mutable tag only
          (apache/spark:3.5.3), not by immutable SHA256 digest.
          A tag can be overwritten in the registry; digest pinning
          (e.g. apache/spark@sha256:<hash>) would guarantee exact
          provenance.

    GAP-4 (IMAGE PULL POLICY): imagePullPolicy is 'IfNotPresent' on
          all containers. If a node already has the image cached, a
          patched upstream image will not be pulled. Setting
          imagePullPolicy to 'Always' (with digest pinning) would
          ensure freshness on each pod start.

    ================================================================
    SECTION 4 — SECURITY CONTEXTS
    ================================================================
    GAP-5 (POD SECURITY CONTEXT — CRITICAL): All workloads
          (spark-master, spark-worker, spark-pi job) have an empty
          pod-level securityContext (securityContext: {}).
          Missing controls:
            - runAsNonRoot: true  (containers may run as root UID 0)
            - runAsUser / runAsGroup  (no UID/GID enforcement)
            - seccompProfile  (no syscall filtering)
            - fsGroup  (no group ownership on mounted volumes)

    GAP-6 (CONTAINER SECURITY CONTEXT — CRITICAL): No container-level
          securityContext is defined on any container in any workload.
          Missing controls:
            - allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            - readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
            - capabilities.drop: ["ALL"]
            - runAsNonRoot: true
          Without these, processes inside containers may escalate
          privileges and have full write access to the container
          filesystem.

    ================================================================
    SECTION 5 — NETWORK POLICIES
    ================================================================
    GAP-7 (NETWORK POLICIES — CRITICAL): Zero NetworkPolicy objects
          exist in the spark-pi namespace. All pods can freely
          initiate connections to and from any pod or service in the
          entire cluster (default-allow). Least-privilege network
          segmentation is completely absent.
          Expected minimum policies:
            - Ingress to spark-master:7077,8080 from spark-worker only
            - Ingress to spark-worker:8081 from spark-master only
            - Egress from spark-submit job to spark-master:7077 only
            - Default-deny-all baseline policy for the namespace

    ================================================================
    SECTION 6 — JOB CONFIGURATION
    ================================================================
    PASS: spark-pi Job has backoffLimit=0 and restartPolicy=Never,
          preventing runaway retry loops on failure.

    PASS: spark-pi Job completed successfully (status=Complete,
          succeeded=1) with no pod restarts.

    PASS: Job pod uses the dedicated 'spark-pi' service account,
          not 'default'.

    ================================================================
    FINDINGS SUMMARY TABLE
    ================================================================
    ID      | Area              | Severity | Finding
    --------|-------------------|----------|---------------------------
    GAP-1   | RBAC              | HIGH     | 'pods' resource added to role post-deploy (annotation drift)
    GAP-2   | Resource Quotas   | MEDIUM   | No LimitRange or ResourceQuota in namespace
    GAP-3   | Image Provenance  | MEDIUM   | Images tagged by name only, no SHA256 digest pinning
    GAP-4   | Image Pull Policy | LOW      | imagePullPolicy=IfNotPresent may serve stale cached images
    GAP-5   | Pod SecCtx        | CRITICAL | Empty pod securityContext on all workloads (root risk)
    GAP-6   | Container SecCtx  | CRITICAL | No container securityContext on any workload (no privilege drop)
    GAP-7   | Network Policy    | CRITICAL | No NetworkPolicies; namespace is fully open (default-allow)

    ================================================================
    END OF AUDIT REPORT
    ================================================================
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: "2026-06-24T10:11:59Z"
  name: config-audit
  namespace: spark-pi
  resourceVersion: "975642"
  uid: 6250e972-ea3e-4874-948f-ffe8fdd586e6

    ================================================================
    PASS: All three workloads (spark-master Deployment, spark-worker
          Deployment, spark-pi Job) define both resource requests and
          limits on every container:
            - CPU:    requests=200m, limits=500m
            - Memory: requests=512Mi, limits=1Gi

    GAP-2 (RESOURCE QUOTAS): No LimitRange or ResourceQuota objects
          exist in the namespace. Without them, any future workload

    ================================================================

          rather than ':latest'. The tag is consistent across all
          three workloads.

    GAP-3 (IMAGE DIGEST): Images are referenced by mutable tag only

          (e.g. apache/spark@sha256:<hash>) would guarantee exact
          provenance.

    GAP-4 (IMAGE PULL POLICY): imagePullPolicy is 'IfNotPresent' on
          all containers. If a node already has the image cached, a
          patched upstream image will not be pulled. Setting
          imagePullPolicy to 'Always' (with digest pinning) would
          ensure freshness on each pod start.

    ================================================================
    SECTION 4 — SECURITY CONTEXTS
    ================================================================
    GAP-5 (POD SECURITY CONTEXT — CRITICAL): All workloads
          (spark-master, spark-worker, spark-pi job) have an empty
          pod-level securityContext (securityContext: {}).
          Missing controls:
            - runAsNonRoot: true  (containers may run as root UID 0)
            - runAsUser / runAsGroup  (no UID/GID enforcement)
            - seccompProfile  (no syscall filtering)
            - fsGroup  (no group ownership on mounted volumes)

    GAP-6 (CONTAINER SECURITY CONTEXT — CRITICAL): No container-level
          securityContext is defined on any container in any workload.
          Missing controls:

content.thinking:
Now I have a comprehensive picture of the spark-pi namespace. Let me analyze what I've found:

1. **Deployments:**
   - `spark-master`: apache/spark:3.5.3, resources limits: cpu=500m/memory=1Gi, requests: cpu=200m/memory=512Mi
   - `spark-worker`: same image and resources, env: SPARK_WORKER_MEMORY=1G, SPARK_WORKER_CORES=1

2. **Job:**
   - `spark-pi`: runs `spark-submit --master local[2] --driver-memory 512m --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi` with 1000 iterations. 

3. **RBAC setup:**
   - Dedicated ServiceAccount `spark-pi` with a custom role that grants permissions for pods, services, configmaps, and persistent volume claims
   - RoleBinding connects the role to the service account

4. **Audit findings:**
   - High priority issue with the pods resource being added to the role beyond the original specification
   - Medium priority gaps around missing LimitRange/ResourceQuota and lack of SHA256 digest pinning on images
   - Low priority concern about imagePullPolicy not being set to Always
   - Three critical security gaps: missing pod-level securityContext, no container-level securityContext, and absence of NetworkPolicies

Now I'm planning out a migration strategy as a ConfigMap that documents the current state, required changes, the sequence to apply them, and verification steps for each. I'll organize the gaps by risk level, starting with the safest changes like removing the pods permission from RBAC, then moving to namespace-level controls like ResourceQuota and LimitRange.

Writing the migration plan...

content.text: I have a complete picture. The `config-audit` ConfigMap already contains a detailed audit. Now I'll write a migration plan that addresses all 7 gaps in a safe, ordered sequence.
